behavior: Highly social and noisy; travels and feeds in flocks, eating seeds, nuts, fruits, and greens in trees or on the ground. Builds bulky communal stick nests with separate chambers and flies directly with rapid wingbeats.
field_marks: Bright-green upperparts with a paler yellow-green belly
Gray forehead, face, throat, and breast with a scalloped texture
Stout, pale-pink hooked bill and dark-brown eye
Pointed green wings with blue outer flight feathers
Large-headed profile with a long, tapered green tail
habitat: Dry, open savannas and grasslands with scattered trees, scrub forest, and palm groves; also adaptable to farmland, orchards, and urban or suburban areas with trees or elevated structures for nesting.

sources.title: Monk Parakeet Identification — All About Birds
